<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="4 of 5 tasks done, only aria-live remains">
Status update — almost done!

- Task #1 (a11y error elements): DONE, CI green
- Task #2 (Forgot password link): DONE, CI green
- Task #3 (HTMX enhancement): DONE, CI green (57 tests)
- Task #5 (Password toggle): DONE, CI green

**One task remaining: Task #4 (aria-live announcements).** Luca is driving.

Everyone else: stand by for final review. Once Task #4 is committed and CI green, all polish work is complete.

Reminder: ONE DRIVER (Luca). Do not modify files.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Acknowledged. Luca is driving Task #4. I'm standing by and will not modify any files.

Let me pull latest so I can review Carson's Task #3 and Lea's Task #5 work while waiting.

content.text: Excellent work from Carson. Let me also quickly check the service layer to confirm domain stayed pure.
Domain and service layers are clean -- no HTMX leakage. Now let me send my consolidated review.

input.content:
Reviewed Task #3 (HTMX, a68f9a2) and Task #5 (password toggle, 649fb5d). Domain architecture assessment:

**Task #3 (HTMX) -- Carson's work is excellent:**
- `is_htmx_request()` helper is in the routes layer only. Domain and service layers have zero HTMX awareness (confirmed via grep).
- `TodoItemTemplate` is a clean fragment template using `TodoItemView` -- the same view model used by the full page. No duplication of domain mapping logic.
- Services now return the domain objects (`add_todo` returns `TodoItem`, `toggle_todo_completion` returns toggled `TodoItem`) so the handler can render fragments. This is the right change -- the service was already doing the work, it just wasn't exposing the result.
- The `todos.html` refactor to always render `<ul class="todo-list">` (with `hidden` when empty) is smart -- it gives HTMX a stable target for `hx-target=".todo-list"` while keeping the empty state visible.
- Progressive enhancement intact: forms still have `method="post"` and `action` attributes, so they work without JS.

**Task #5 (password toggle) -- clean progressive enhancement:**
- TypeScript in `static/js/password-toggle.js` -- purely additive, no domain impact.
- Only on register.html for now (makes sense for MVP -- that's where `new-password` fields are).

**No domain concerns on either commit.** The architecture is holding up well -- presentation changes stay in routes/templates, domain stays pure.

Standing by for Task #4 review when Luca pushes.
input.summary: Tasks #3 and #5 reviewed, domain clean, approved

routing.content:
Reviewed Task #3 (HTMX, a68f9a2) and Task #5 (password toggle, 649fb5d). Domain architecture assessment:

**Task #3 (HTMX) -- Carson's work is excellent:**
- `is_htmx_request()` helper is in the routes layer only. Domain and service layers have zero HTMX awareness (confirmed via grep).
- `TodoItemTemplate` is a clean fragment template using `TodoItemView` -- the same view model used by the full page. No duplication of domain mapping logic.
- Services now return the domain objects (`add_todo` returns `TodoItem`, `toggle_todo_completion` returns toggled `TodoItem`) so the handler can render fragments. This is the right change -- the service was already doing the work, it just wasn't exposing the result.
- The `todos.html` refactor to always render `<ul class="todo-list">` (with `hidden` when empty) is smart -- it gives HTMX a stable target for `hx-target=".todo-list"` while keeping the empty state visible.
- Progressive enhancement intact: forms still have `method="post"` and `action` attributes, so they work without JS.

**Task #5 (password toggle) -- clean progressive enhancement:**
- TypeScript in `static/js/password-toggle.js` -- purely additive, no domain impact.
- Only on register.html for now (makes sense for MVP -- that's where `new-password` fields are).

**No domain concerns on either commit.** The architecture is holding up well -- presentation changes stay in routes/templates, domain stays pure.

Standing by for Task #4 review when Luca pushes.
